metadata.title: Every Remotion Move, One Logo.
metadata.greeting_note: hello lexicon: Amharic. claude-liam channel. Liam in for Bear.
metadata.note: Technique-showcase reel: the Musinique logo-2 mark animated every way Remotion knows. 22 technique beats + bookends. Audio-first, free pipeline only (Kokoro am_onyx). No ElevenLabs, no Higgsfield, no publishing. Subject: logos/musinique-logo-2.svg, viewBox 0 0 2048 2048, square format, complex multi-path music brand mark.

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. One logo. Twenty techniques. I am going to run the Musinique mark through every motion move Remotion knows, so you can see which ones actually deserve to live.
Spring Entrance. Default spring config, pop-in from nothing. Watch how it settles — is there enough weight, or does it feel cheap?
Overshoot Spring. High stiffness, low damping — squash and stretch on landing. Feels physical, or just noisy?
Draw-On Stroke. The path traces itself with evolve path, then the fill floods in. Pen, or just a loading bar with delusions?
Mask Reveal. Linear wipe left to right, then radial iris open. Two reveals, one beat — the world opens in phases.
Scale Zoom. Linear scale first half, bezier easing second half. You can feel the curve earn its keep in the second pass.
Rotation. Spring entrance pivots in, then slow continuous hold. Meditative at low speed, lazy at high — pick your read.
Skew And Shear. Spring in, lean hard, hold, release. That shear tension is either dynamic energy or a geometry mistake — the hold decides.
Opacity Through Blur. Arrives out of focus, snap to sharp. The blur is not decoration — it is proof of distance traveled.
Color Interpolation. Ink to terracotta and back. This is the treatment beat — the one time in the reel we let the palette breathe.
Kinetic Grid. The mark tiled across the frame, staggered ripple entrance. Grid becomes rhythm when the delay is right.
Glitch Slices. Horizontal slices, random per-frame offsets, chromatic hint. Aggressive for six frames, gone. That's the correct duration.
Trail Echo. Mark slides across frame, ghost copies follow with decaying opacity. Motion lingers — that is the whole argument for this technique.
Noise Wobble. Sine-driven x, y, and rotation offset, amplitude decaying over the beat. Alive in the first second, settled by the last.
Elastic Physics. Drop from above, squash on landing, multiple rebounds. Low damping, higher stiffness — it hits the floor and knows it.
Card Flip. Perspective rotate-Y spring entrance, then slow second rotation. The depth plane is not free — you pay for it with complexity.
Shadow Play. Independent shadow offset layer stretches ahead, then snaps back. Detached from the mark, grounded again.
Composer Summon. The mark typed into Claude, send button fires a terracotta spark, and the logo arrives. The interface is the origin.
Stroke Pulse. The path stroke-dashoffset cycles continuously — a heartbeat rhythm. The mark is alive, not just rendered.
Scale Breathe. Very slow continuous scale oscillation — barely perceptible, never still. The quietest technique in this reel.
Exit Family. Three exits back to back: shrink-spin, blur-out, mask-close iris. Exits are underrated — they are the last thing the viewer remembers.
Your turn. Take your own logo SVG and run it through spring entrance, draw-on stroke, and the exit family. For each technique, build a six-second Remotion composition and mark it keep or kill.
Liam, in for Bear.
